title: schoenoplectus lacustris subsp. tabernaemontani
intro: Schoenoplectus lacustris subsp. tabernaemontani, also known as common cattail or great cattail, is a perennial aquatic plant native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Schoenoplectus lacustris subsp. tabernaemontani include its ability to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
